sections: Sea Freight Or Air Freight — Choosing The Right Route

The single biggest decision on any international move is how your belongings actually travel, and for most households the honest answer is sea freight. It is the most cost-effective way to move the volume a typical home generates — furniture, white goods, boxed kitchens, wardrobes of clothing and the accumulated possessions of a family. Sea freight trades a longer transit time for a far gentler price per cubic metre, which is why it remains the backbone of nearly every worldwide relocation we handle from Sussex. Air freight, by contrast, is fast but priced by weight, so it suits a small, urgent shipment — documents you need quickly, a handful of essentials to tide you over, or a tight overlap where you simply cannot wait for a vessel. Many clients use the two together: the bulk of the home goes by sea while a modest air consignment travels ahead so you are not living out of a suitcase for weeks.

We talk you through the trade-offs before anything is booked, because the right answer depends on your timeline, your budget and exactly what you are shipping. If your move is within the continent rather than truly worldwide, our European removals service may be the better and quicker road-based option. For everything further afield, the comparison below is the starting point for an honest plan.

  • Sea freight — best value for whole-home volumes; longer transit; priced by space used
  • Air freight — fastest but priced by weight; ideal for small, time-critical consignments
  • Combined — essentials by air, the rest by sea, so you settle in sooner

Once sea freight is chosen, the next question is how your goods are loaded into the container, and this is where careful advice saves real money. A sole-use (full container load) shipment dedicates an entire container to your move alone. Your possessions are loaded at your home, sealed, and not touched again until they reach the destination — the quickest, most direct and most protective option, and the natural choice when you have a full house to move. A shared or groupage (less-than-container-load) shipment places your goods alongside other consignments heading to the same region, so you pay only for the space you genuinely occupy. For a part-home move, a flat clearance or a smaller volume, groupage can be dramatically cheaper.

The trade-off is timing and handling: a shared container waits to be filled and is consolidated and de-consolidated at warehouses along the way, so transit takes longer and your goods are handled more often. We are upfront about which option fits your shipment, because recommending a sole-use container for a half-house move — or cramming a full home into groupage and watching the schedule slip — helps nobody. When you request your international removals quote we size your inventory honestly so the container choice matches the reality of what you are sending.
Export Packing Standards That Survive The Journey

International transit is unforgiving. Your belongings face being lifted by crane, stacked in a hold, exposed to temperature swings and humidity at sea, and handled by multiple teams across thousands of miles. Domestic wrapping that copes with a van trip across Sussex is simply not built for that, which is why everything destined to go overseas is prepared to a proper export packing standard. We use double-walled cartons, generous cushioning, moisture-resistant materials and corner protection so each item is insulated against vibration, pressure and the damp marine environment. Furniture is blanket-wrapped and then over-wrapped; mirrors, glass and artwork are sandwiched in purpose-made protection; electronics are cushioned against shock.

For high-value, fragile or awkward pieces — antiques, pianos, marble tops, large mirrors or sculpture — we build a bespoke timber crate measured to the item, giving it a rigid shell that protects it through every lift and stack. As a LAPADA member we handle fine furniture and antiques to the standard those pieces deserve. Where you would rather not lift a finger, our full packing service takes care of the entire home, packing every room to export grade and producing the detailed inventory your shipment needs.

  • Double-walled export cartons and moisture-resistant wrapping throughout
  • Blanket-wrapped, over-wrapped furniture and protected fragile items
  • Bespoke crating for antiques, art, pianos and awkward pieces
  • Detailed inventory created as we pack, ready for customs

Every international move travels on its paperwork, and an accurate, properly itemised inventory is the document that holds the whole shipment together. As your goods are packed we build a numbered inventory listing each carton and item, which serves three jobs at once: it tells you exactly what has been sent, it underpins your insurance cover, and it forms the basis of the customs declaration at the destination. Vague or missing inventories are one of the most common reasons a shipment is delayed or held for inspection, so we take the time to get this right at the point of packing rather than scrambling for it later.

We guide you through the documentation each move typically requires and help you prepare it cleanly, working alongside trusted destination agents who handle clearance at the other end. Customs requirements differ from country to country and change over time, so we keep our guidance general and route-specific advice to the appropriate agent — what we promise is that the paperwork leaving Sussex is complete, accurate and presented in the way clearance teams expect. That groundwork is what keeps a worldwide move moving. You can read more about our family-run team and the experience we bring to getting these details right.
What You Can — And Can’t — Ship

Part of planning a worldwide move is knowing early what should and should not travel, because finding out at the dock is the expensive way to learn. Most of an ordinary household ships without difficulty: furniture, clothing, books, kitchenware, white goods and personal effects are all routine. Certain categories, however, are restricted or prohibited almost everywhere and are far better dealt with before packing day. Foodstuffs, plants, seeds and soil are commonly controlled because of biosecurity rules; aerosols, fuels, paints, gas bottles and other flammables are hazardous and generally excluded from shipping; and items such as firearms, certain timbers or anything covered by wildlife protection rules need special handling or simply cannot go.

Rather than quote you specific rules for a specific country — which change and vary — we flag the broad categories to set aside and help you plan around them well in advance, confirming the detail with the destination agent. Anything you are leaving behind, sending later, or holding while you settle in can go into our secure storage here in Sussex, so your shipment carries only what should genuinely make the journey.

  • Set aside food, plants, seeds and soil — commonly restricted on biosecurity grounds
  • Never ship aerosols, fuels, paints, gas bottles or other flammables
  • Flag firearms, certain timbers and protected items early for special handling
  • Use UK storage for anything not travelling with the main shipment
Destination Delivery, Unpacking & Storage At Either End

A worldwide move is only finished when your belongings are inside your new home, not when the container leaves port. Through our network of established destination agents we arrange final delivery to your address overseas, including unloading, placement of furniture in the rooms you choose and, where you want it, full unpacking and removal of the empty cartons so you are not left wading through packaging in an unfamiliar house. The same care we take wrapping your home in Sussex carries through to the team setting it back up at the other end.

Timing rarely lines up perfectly, and that is normal. If your new home is not ready when the shipment arrives, storage at the destination can hold your goods until it is. Equally, if you need to leave the UK before everything is packed and shipped, our own storage facility near Pulborough can hold your possessions securely until the right vessel and the right delivery window come together. Storage at either end turns the trickiest part of any international move — the gap between leaving one home and entering the next — into something genuinely manageable.
faqs: How far in advance should I book an international move?

The earlier the better — we generally recommend starting the conversation several weeks ahead, and more if your home is large or your destination is remote. Sea freight runs to fixed sailing schedules, export packing and crating take time to do properly, and documentation needs preparing before anything leaves. Booking early gives us room to choose the right vessel and container option rather than the only one left. Request a quote as soon as you have a rough timeline and we will map out the dates with you.
Should I send everything by sea, or use air freight too?

For most households the bulk goes by sea because it is far better value for the volume a home generates, while air freight suits a small, urgent consignment of essentials you need straight away. Many clients combine the two so they are not waiting weeks for basics. We assess your inventory and timeline and recommend the split that genuinely fits — there is no one-size answer, which is why we plan it with you rather than guess.
Do you handle the customs paperwork for me?

We prepare a detailed, accurate inventory as we pack and guide you through the documentation your move typically needs, then work with trusted destination agents who handle clearance at the other end. Because customs requirements differ by country and change over time, we keep our own guidance general and route the precise detail through the appropriate agent — what we guarantee is that the paperwork leaving Sussex is complete and presented the way clearance teams expect.
Are my belongings covered while they travel overseas?

Yes. We are a fully insured, Checkatrade-verified and LAPADA-member company carrying £10m liability cover, and your itemised inventory underpins the cover for your shipment. We will talk you through the protection in place for an international move before you commit, so you understand exactly how your goods are covered from collection in Sussex through to delivery at your new home. You can reach us on 01903 893731 or contact@wolves-removals.co.uk to discuss the detail.
